mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or FEDFUNDS fails to correlate with SPX returns in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 60d windows following GDPC1 growth episodes (t-test p > 0.05), or GDPC1 shows no predictive power in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 3.5%) creates stagflationary risks that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of stagflationary episodes, or interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS lacks statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 4%) signals tight labor markets that boost corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 20d windows during low UNRATE periods (t-test p > 0.05), or UNRATE shows no predictive power in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 60%+ of INDPRO growth periods, or INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST > 2500 thousand units) indicates strong demand for construction, which boosts related sectors and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of HOUST surge periods, or HOUST shows no predictive power in 1-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) indicates a tightening labor market that drives corporate wage pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of labor market tightness episodes, or interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE lacks statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS > 360 billion) indicates resilient demand, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 60d windows following RSAFS growth episodes (t-test p > 0.05), or RSAFS shows no predictive power in 3-year rolling regressions.
